story: The day I saw the hit-and-run, I recognized the boy at the wheel—Parker Sumter, my ninth-grade student. I phoned police and said I hadn’t seen the driver. Mr. Bradshaw died. Years passed; Parker graduated; I told no one. After six years, Parker has kept clippings in a drawer and, today, intends to walk into the county station after work and confess. After my last class, the sky is blank and gray. I stand by my car outside the school.
implausible_knowledge: After six years, Parker has kept clippings in a drawer and, today, intends to walk into the county station after work and confess.
question: After school, what will she most likely do about the hit-and-run?
answer_with_knowledge: Drive to the county station to offer a statement.
